assertion_results.text: Agent uses --format=json or --format='json(name,zone.basename())' or similar JSON projection syntax
assertion_results.evidence: Line 48: --format="json(name,zone.basename(),project)" — JSON output with field projection using basename() transform.

assertion_results.text: Agent uses --filter to filter by machine type containing n1-standard-1
assertion_results.evidence: Line 47: --filter="machineType:n1-standard-1" — server-side filter using substring match operator.

assertion_results.text: Agent mentions the cross-project challenge and suggests gcloud asset search-all-resources or iterating over projects with gcloud projects list
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 59-91: iterating over projects via 'gcloud projects list --format="value(projectId)"'. Lines 134-153: 'gcloud asset search-all-resources' as alternative for large orgs.

assertion_results.text: Agent shows how to use --format='value(field)' or --format='table(field)' for extracting specific fields
assertion_results.evidence: Line 29: --format="value(projectId)" used to extract raw project IDs for scripting. Key flags table (lines 173-179) documents both value() and json() format patterns.

assertion_results.text: Agent mentions jq as a post-processing tool for transforming or filtering gcloud JSON output
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 79-108: extensive use of jq — 'jq --arg proj "$PROJECT" [.[] | {name, zone, project: $proj}]' and 'jq -s add // []' with detailed explanation of the pipeline.

assertion_results.text: Agent demonstrates at least one concrete --format example with field projection (not just --format=json which dumps everything)
assertion_results.evidence: Line 48: --format="json(name,zone.basename(),project)" — concrete field projection with basename() transform, not a raw json dump.

assertion_results.text: All commands shown are read-only (list, describe, search) and agent runs them without asking for confirmation
assertion_results.evidence: All commands use 'list', 'projects list', or 'asset search-all-resources'. Lines 19-20: 'Every command below is read-only. No confirmation is needed. Execute freely.'

assertion_results.text: Agent does NOT suggest creating or modifying any resources
assertion_results.evidence: No create, update, delete, or modify commands appear anywhere in the response. All operations are purely read-only queries.
